content_markdown:
The first law of thermodynamics may be expressed in terms of the quantities below.

$\Delta U$, the **increase** in the internal energy of the system
$Q$, the energy transferred **to** the system by heating
$W$, the work done **on** the system

Which **one** of the following is a correct statement of the law?

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram present, only text and mathematical equations.

vibe_explanation: The first law of thermodynamics is a statement of energy conservation. The change in internal energy ($\Delta U$) is equal to the heat added to the system ($Q$) plus the work done on the system ($W$). Rearranging $\Delta U = Q + W$ to solve for $W$ gives $W = \Delta U - Q$.
